title: City of Vienna Digital Infrastructure
summary: Independent application security assessment conducted through the City of Vienna's public Bugcrowd program. The engagement identified multiple accepted information disclosure findings and demonstrated how individually low-severity issues can combine into meaningful attack chains.

timeline.summary: Reconnaissance, authentication analysis and attack surface mapping completed.
Validated four independent information disclosure findings while confirming cryptographic controls remained effective.
Submitted findings through the Bugcrowd managed bug bounty program.
All submissions accepted by Bugcrowd triage as valid informational findings.
Engagement concluded after triage and documentation.

confidentiality.summary: Information disclosure reduced attacker effort by exposing internal implementation details.

privacy.summary: No personal data exposure was demonstrated.

regulatory.summary: Findings support GDPR defense-in-depth objectives but did not demonstrate regulatory non-compliance.

operational.summary: Collective disclosures reduced the effort required to understand internal architecture and potential attack paths.

lessons.summary: Information disclosure findings should be evaluated as components of larger attack chains rather than in isolation.
Demonstrating attacker impact is often more important than simply identifying exposed information.
Strong cryptographic controls successfully prevented exploitation despite supporting information disclosures.
Verbose error messages significantly accelerate attacker reconnaissance.

seo.description: Independent application security assessment of the City of Vienna's public digital infrastructure conducted through the Bugcrowd managed bug bounty program.
